title: Box Office: Avatar: Fire And Ash Bests 28 Years Later: Bone Temple with $17M+
text: Avatar: Fire and Ash retained its box office dominance over the four-day MLK weekend, earning approximately $17.8 million, maintaining the number one position. Meanwhile, 28 Years Later: The Bone Temple came in behind with an estimated $15 million over the same period. This represented a significant shortfall from the $20-22 million that tracking had suggested before the film's release. The sequel earned $13 million over the three-day weekend frame. With a $63 million production budget, the film's underperformance is particularly concerning for Sony's profitability calculations. Standard industry benchmarks require box office returns roughly 2.5 times the production budget for profitability. For The Bone Temple, that threshold sits at $157.5 million worldwide. Industry analysts note that without exceptionally strong word-of-mouth driving legs, the film faces an uphill battle to reach break-even. The film's strong critical reception (92% on Rotten Tomatoes) and A- CinemaScore suggest potential for stronger holds in subsequent weekends.
